prose:
Frontier orbital calculation on formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms, 8 occupied MOs, 74 basis functions) using DFT at tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied .xyz, used as a single-point (not re-optimised at DFT level). SCF converged in 8 cycles. No warnings reported. Integrity check: trustworthy=true, all checks passed.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference).
HOMO (index 7) = −7.6523067 eV; LUMO (index 8) = −1.7826355 eV.

Koopmans-theorem derived quantities: vertical IP = 7.6523 eV, vertical EA = 1.7826 eV, electronegativity = 4.7175 eV, chemical hardness = 2.9348 eV, softness = 0.34073 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity index = 3.7914 eV.

Total electronic energy: −114.55325 Hartree (−3117.1528 eV).

Caveats: (1) Density fitting (RI) is on by default — an approximation to the two-electron integrals. (2) The gap is a Koopmans orbital gap, not an optical excitation energy (TD-DFT would be needed for that). (3) Single-point on user-supplied geometry — no DFT-level re-optimisation. (4) No warnings from engine (empty warnings array — clean run).
